rulings.Case Title: EST OF JANE ALTER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hermanns Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate, Correct And Amend The Order Of Final Distribution Confirming And Settling Fourth Report And Account Of Administrator
rulings.Rulings: Hearing required. (1) What is the ground under CCP section 663 to vacate or amend this order? If the Court concludes there was not an incorrect or erroneous legal basis for the decision, and it was not inconsistent with the facts, is it enough that the parties agree that the Court should revisit its order? Does Probate Code section 7220 apply to this motion? (2) Assuming the Court can reach the merits, is the administrator now taking the position that she would prefer not being directed to distribute prior to there being a final determination of the controversy over attorney fees? Does the language of the order already allow the administrator to distribute only after that dispute has been resolved? Does the order leave discretion to the administrator to decide how to deal with the notice of lien? Would the administrator's concern be addressed by deleting the words "directs and" from paragraph 7 of the order? (3) Given the applicable law appoarently requiring the fee issues to be decided in a separate action and the fact that the dispute is already pending in a different court, on what basis could or should this Court accept the administrator's invitation to decide the dispute over the validity of the claimed lien or the amount, if any, owed to the attorneys? (4) If this Court is not going to resolve the fee controversy, what is the administrator's preferred outcome on this motion?

If the Court concludes there was not an incorrect or erroneous legal basis for the decision, and it was not inconsistent with the facts, is it enough that the parties agree that the Court should revisit its order? Does Probate Code section 7220 apply to this motion? (2) Assuming the Court can reach the merits, is the administrator now taking the position that she would prefer not being directed to distribute prior to there being a final determination of the controversy over attorney fees? (4) If this Court is not going to resolve the fee controversy, what is the administrator's preferred outcome on this motion?
